digest.component_or_area: enrichment opportunistic detached drain + rebar enrich CLI
propositions: A cheap maybe_drain() gate on ordinary write invocations no-ops when nothing is soaked and otherwise detaches enrichment to a child process that outlives the command.

Detach is POSIX-only in v1 (start_new_session); the Windows DETACHED_PROCESS|CREATE_NO_WINDOW branch is authored but maybe_drain no-ops on Windows because lock.py imports fcntl unconditionally and would crash a Windows drain child.

The drainer loop claims soaked+unclaimed tickets via optimistic queue.claim, runs enrich, writes TICKET_DIGEST, appends DONE_ENRICH, and prunes queue events keeping only the latest (keep=1) to bound log growth.

A best-effort advisory lock file .rebar/enrich-drain.lock prevents concurrent drain processes; a second drain with it held exits 0 silently without blocking, distinct from the store write lock and the optimistic queue claim.
The rebar enrich CLI provides --drain (bounded detached target), --once (in-process single entry), and status (prints JSON pending/claimed/soaking bucket counts from the queue reducer).

overlap_drain (off|async|always, default async), overlap_drain_batch, and overlap_drain_gate_budget_ms are LLMConfig fields read via from_env, with invalid overlap_drain values falling back to default; overlap_drain='always' runs the drain synchronously inline.
